text: When you write code, bugs are almost certain to appear. Learning to fix them is an essential skill. The first step is identifying the bug by observing unexpected behaviour. Instead of guessing, take a systematic approach: isolate the problem, form a hypothesis, and test it step by step. Many programmers use debugging tools like breakpoints to pause code and inspect variables. Simple logging, such as adding `print()` statements, can also reveal what’s happening at each stage. Carefully reading error messages helps pinpoint the issue. A calm, logical method saves time and frustration, making debugging a productive part of the development process.
